subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The capacity of a mixing tank must be authorised by the Permit Authority.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A mixing tank and baffles must be constructed of a minimum—

ol.li: 0.63 mm acid resistant stainless steel all welded construction; or
other authorised acid and alkali resistant material.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A mixing tank must contain three equally spaced baffles.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A mixing tank pipe diameter must be—

ol.li: DN 100 when the pipe inlet size is DN 100; or
a minimum of one size larger than the inlet pipe.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The outlet of a mixing tank must include a 32 mm x 32 mm x 25 mm deep PVC-U test block bracket fitted as near as practicable to the outlet.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A mixing tank must include a 150 mm x 25 mm x 25 mm cement mortar test block fitted to the satisfaction of the Permit Authority.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A mixing tank must be located in a position that—

ol.li: enables ready access for maintenance and inspection; and
provides ready access for fitting of the cement mortar test block; and
enables ready access to the sampling valve.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: When a mixing tank is to be located below ground, it must—

ol.li: be surrounded with concrete walls and base with a minimum thickness of 100 mm; and
omit the 25 mm sampling valve; and
have a minimum DN 100 outlet pipe located no deeper than 600 mm below surface level; and
have the outlet pipe connected to a disconnector gully fitted with a loose fitting grate or screw cap.

callout.p: The minimum capacity requirement of a typical mixing tank in accordance with C4D18(1) is 100 litres.
See TAS Figure C4D18 for a typical mixing tank.
Consideration should be given to a mixing tank being provided with adequate structural support.
